title: King James Version
description: Commissioned by King James I and first published in 1611, produced by 47 scholars from the Church of England. A word-for-word translation from the Textus Receptus and Masoretic Text, and one of the most influential English translations in history.

title: American Standard Version
description: Published in 1901 as the American revision of the English Revised Version, known for its rigorous word-for-word literal accuracy. It uses 'Jehovah' instead of 'LORD' for the divine name and was widely regarded as one of the most literal English translations of its era.

title: Darby Bible
description: Translated by John Nelson Darby, a founder of the Plymouth Brethren movement, published in its final revised edition in 1890. An extremely literal word-for-word translation known for its precise rendering of Greek and Hebrew verb tenses.

title: Young's Literal Translation
description: Translated by Robert Young, a Scottish publisher and biblical scholar, with its third and final edition published in 1898. One of the most strictly literal English translations ever produced, preserving Hebrew and Greek verb tenses with extreme precision.

title: Geneva Bible
description: Produced by English Protestant exiles in Geneva and first published in 1557, it was the first English Bible to use verse numbering throughout and included extensive Calvinist study notes. The most popular English Bible before the KJV, used by Shakespeare and the Pilgrims.

title: The Bishop's Bible
description: Commissioned by the Church of England under Archbishop Matthew Parker in 1568, intended to replace the Geneva Bible as the authorized church version. It served as the primary basis for the King James Version of 1611.

title: Tyndale Bible
description: The first English Bible translated directly from Hebrew and Greek sources, produced by William Tyndale around 1530. A pioneering literal translation that profoundly influenced nearly every subsequent English Bible, including the King James Version.

title: Coverdale Bible
description: The first complete printed English Bible, translated by Miles Coverdale in 1535 from Latin and German sources including Tyndale's and Luther's work. It introduced the arrangement of grouping the Apocrypha as a separate section between the Old and New Testaments.

title: World English Bible
description: A modern public-domain translation based on the ASV (1901), updated into contemporary English by volunteers led by Michael Paul Johnson. A literal translation and one of the few modern translations freely available without copyright restrictions.

title: World English Bible (British Edition)
description: A variant of the World English Bible adapted to use British English spelling and vocabulary conventions. It retains the same literal translation philosophy and public-domain status as the standard WEB.

title: New Heart English Bible
description: A public-domain translation derived from the World English Bible, edited by Wayne A. Mitchell to remove archaic language and produce a clear, readable modern English text. Follows a modified literal approach and is freely available without copyright restrictions.

title: Unlocked Literal Bible
description: A literal English translation published by unfoldingWord in 2017, designed as an open-licensed resource for missionaries and translators to freely use as a base text for translating the Bible into other languages.

title: Unlocked Dynamic Bible
description: A thought-for-thought English translation published by unfoldingWord in 2018, intended as a companion to the ULB to help translators understand the meaning of the biblical text. Released under an open Creative Commons license.

title: Wycliffe Bible
description: One of the earliest English translations of the complete Bible, produced by John Wycliffe and his associates around 1382-1395, translated from the Latin Vulgate. A landmark in making Scripture accessible to English-speaking laity during the late medieval period, written in Middle English.

file_name: Bíblia de João Ferreira de Almeida - Edição de 1819.SQLite3
title: Bíblia de João Ferreira de Almeida - Edição de 1819
description: An 1819 edition of the Portuguese Bible originally translated by João Ferreira de Almeida, a 17th-century Protestant minister who produced the first complete Portuguese translation from the original languages. One of the most widely used Portuguese Bibles, following a word-for-word approach.
download_link: https://github.com/JenuelDev/Believers-Sword/raw/main/Modules/Bible/Bíblia de João Ferreira de Almeida - Edição de 1819.SQLite3

title: Catholic Public Domain Version 2009
description: An English translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate published in 2009 by Ronald L. Conte Jr., produced from a Catholic perspective and including the deuterocanonical books. Released in the public domain, it aims for a literal yet readable rendering.

file_name: King James Bible 1611 Pure Cambridge Edition 1900.SQLite3
title: King James Bible 1611 Pure Cambridge Edition 1900
description: The Pure Cambridge Edition, finalized around 1900 by Cambridge University Press, representing centuries of careful corrections to the original 1611 KJV text. Widely regarded by KJV purists as the most accurate and consistent printed form of the Authorized Version.

title: King James Version - 1769
description: The 1769 revision edited by Benjamin Blayney at Oxford, which modernized spelling and punctuation of the original 1611 text. This revision is effectively the standard KJV text in common use today, based on the Textus Receptus and Masoretic Text.

title: King James Version of 1611 or 1769
description: The Authorized Version commissioned by King James I, first published in 1611 by 47 scholars, with the 1769 Oxford revision by Benjamin Blayney being the form most widely circulated today. A word-for-word translation from the Textus Receptus and Masoretic Text, renowned for its literary influence.
